    "title": "feat(pwa): add install orientation setting",
    "user": {
      "login": "vhqtvn",
      "id": 8930337,
      "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjg5MzAzMzc=",
      "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8930337?v=4",
      "gravatar_id": "",

    "body": "## Summary\r\n\r\nAdds a configurable PWA install orientation setting for OpenChamber.\r\n\r\nThis change wires the setting through:\r\n- Visual settings UI\r\n- Persisted settings handling\r\n- Runtime manifest generation for the web app\r\n\r\nUsers can now choose whether the installed PWA should:\r\n- Follow system rotation\r\n- Lock to portrait\r\n- Lock to landscape\r\n\r\n## Changes\r\n\r\n- Adds a new `Install Orientation` setting in OpenChamber visual settings\r\n- Persists `pwaOrientation` in settings\r\n- Updates the client-side manifest refresh flow to include orientation\r\n- Updates the server manifest route to emit the correct manifest orientation\r\n- Keeps the default behavior as `system`\r\n\r\n## Notes\r\n\r\n- The setting only applies to the installed web app / PWA\r\n- Users may need to reinstall the PWA after changing the setting for the new orientation to take effect",

    "locked": false,
    "title": "fix: default model silently falls back for providers with slash-containing model IDs (OpenRouter, LM Studio, Google)",
    "user": {
      "login": "bk-bf",
      "id": 106319367,
      "node_id": "U_kgDOBlZOBw",
      "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/106319367?v=4",
      "gravatar_id": "",

    "body": "Closes #896\n\n## Summary\n\n- **Fix default model parsing for slash-containing model IDs** — all 7 call sites that used `split('/')` with a `length === 2` guard now split on the first slash only via `indexOf('/')`. OpenRouter models like `qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b` are stored as `openrouter/qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b` — three segments — which the old guard silently rejected, falling back to the free-tier model.\n- **Fix unguarded `indexOf` on `providerId` prop** — `AgentsPage` and `CommandsPage` were missing the `-1` guard on the `providerId` side; a slash-free model string would produce `slice(0, -1)` and strip the last character silently.\n- **Fix worktree `node_modules` resolution** — on worktree creation, `linkWorktreeNodeModules()` now symlinks the primary worktree's `node_modules` into the linked worktree, so `desktop:dev` and all build scripts work correctly from any worktree without a separate `bun install`.\n- **`desktop:dev` local Vite config support** — `dev-web-server.mjs` now passes `--config vite.config.local.ts` to Vite when that file exists in `packages/web`, allowing per-developer local overrides without modifying tracked config.\n\n## Changes\n\n| File | Change |\n|------|--------|\n| `packages/ui/src/stores/useConfigStore.ts` | `parseModelString()`: split on first slash |\n| `packages/ui/src/components/sections/openchamber/DefaultsSettings.tsx` | `getDisplayModel()`: split on first slash |\n| `packages/ui/src/components/session/NewWorktreeDialog.tsx` | `resolveDefaultModelSelection()`: split on first slash |\n| `packages/ui/src/components/session/GitHubIssuePickerDialog.tsx` | `resolveDefaultModelSelection()`: split on first slash |\n| `packages/ui/src/lib/worktreeSessionCreator.ts` | `applyDefaultAgentAndModelSelection()`: split on first slash |\n| `packages/ui/src/components/sections/agents/AgentsPage.tsx` | `ModelSelector` props: split on first slash + guard `providerId` against missing slash |\n| `packages/ui/src/components/sections/commands/CommandsPage.tsx` | `ModelSelector` props: split on first slash + guard `providerId` against missing slash |\n| `packages/web/server/lib/git/service.js` | `linkWorktreeNodeModules()`: symlink node_modules on worktree creation |\n| `packages/desktop/scripts/dev-web-server.mjs` | Pass `--config vite.config.local.ts` to Vite when the file exists |\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [ ] Set `qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b` (or any OpenRouter model with a `/` in its ID) as the default in Settings → Defaults — selector shows the correct model after saving\n- [ ] Start a new session — the configured default model is applied, not the free-tier fallback\n- [ ] Create a new worktree — the default model is applied to the new worktree session\n- [ ] Open Settings → Agents and Settings → Commands — ModelSelector shows the correct provider/model for any saved OpenRouter model\n- [ ] Run `bun run type-check` and `bun run lint` — both pass",

    "body": "Close #840 \r\n\r\n## Summary\r\n\r\n- Fixes #840 — the `/compact` command appeared to freeze the session with no visual feedback\r\n- Adds `compactSession()` to `session-actions.ts` using the same optimistic message pattern as `optimisticSend()`, but adapted for the summarize API which has no `messageID` parameter\r\n\r\n## Problem\r\n\r\nWhen a user ran `/compact`, `ChatInput.tsx` called `sdk.session.summarize()` directly and returned. There was **no optimistic message**, **no busy indicator**, and **no immediate UI feedback**. The session looked stuck until SSE events eventually arrived — sometimes after a long wait, sometimes only visible after restarting.\r\n\r\n## Approach\r\n\r\nThe fix mirrors `optimisticSend()` but accounts for a key difference: the summarize API doesn't accept a `messageID`, so the server generates its own message IDs for compaction messages.\r\n\r\n**Strategy:**\r\n1. Insert an optimistic user message (`/compact`) for instant visual feedback\r\n2. Set session status to `busy` so the UI shows activity\r\n3. Call `sdk.session.summarize()` — server runs the compaction agent\r\n4. After the API resolves, **remove the optimistic placeholder** to avoid a duplicate (optimistic + real server message)\r\n5. SSE events (`message.updated`, `message.part.updated`, `session.idle`) deliver the actual compaction result and clear the busy status\r\n\r\n**Why not reuse `optimisticSend()` directly?** `optimisticSend` passes the client-generated `messageID` to the server via `promptAsync({ messageID })` so both share the same ID. The summarize API has no such parameter — the server always generates its own IDs. Keeping the optimistic message after the API resolves would cause a visible duplicate user message.\r\n\r\n## Files changed\r\n\r\n| File | Change |\r\n|------|--------|\r\n| `packages/ui/src/sync/session-actions.ts` | Add `compactSession()` with optimistic message, busy status, and post-resolve cleanup |\r\n| `packages/ui/src/sync/index.ts` | Export `compactSession` |\r\n| `packages/ui/src/components/chat/ChatInput.tsx` | Replace direct `sdk.session.summarize()` call with `sessionActions.compactSession()` |\r\n\r\n## Test plan\r\n\r\n- [ ] Run `/compact` in a session with enough context — user message appears immediately, busy indicator shows, compaction result appears after completion\r\n- [ ] Run `/compact` when server is unreachable — optimistic message is removed, error is surfaced, session returns to idle\r\n- [ ] Verify no duplicate user messages appear after compaction completes\r\n- [ ] Verify session status returns to idle after compaction agent finishes (via `session.idle` SSE event)",

    "locked": false,
    "title": "fix: update parent session tool parts when subagent completes",
    "user": {
      "login": "jwcrystal",
      "id": 121911854,
      "node_id": "U_kgDOB0Q6Lg",
      "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/121911854?v=4",
      "gravatar_id": "",
      "url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal",
      "html_url": "https://github.com/jwcrystal",
      "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal/followers",
      "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal/following{/other_user}",
      "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal/gists{/gist_id}",
      "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal/starred{/owner}{/repo}",
      "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal/subscriptions",
      "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal/orgs",
      "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal/repos",
      "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal/events{/privacy}",
      "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jwcrystal/received_events",
      "type": "User",
      "user_view_type": "public",
      "site_admin": false
    },
    "body": "Fixes #810 (follow-up to #817)\r\n\r\n## Problem\r\n\r\nParent session tool parts don't update when child sessions complete:\r\n\r\n1. **SSE reconnect**: Child session data is resynced, but parent session tool parts aren't\r\n2. **Normal SSE + coalesced events**: When `message.part.updated` events are coalesced or delayed, tool parts remain stuck showing \"waiting for subagent activity\"\r\n\r\n## Solution\r\n\r\n| Scenario | Fix |\r\n|----------|-----|\r\n| SSE reconnect | Sync parent sessions in `resyncDirectoryAfterReconnect` after child sessions are resynced |\r\n| Normal SSE + delayed events | Add `useEffect` in ToolPart that fetches parent session's latest parts when child polling stops, scoped to only the target message containing the task tool part |\r\n\r\n### Fix 1: SSE reconnect scenario\r\nEnhanced `resyncDirectoryAfterReconnect` in `sync-context.tsx`:\r\n- Track all child sessions that were resynced\r\n- Find their parent sessions and resync their messages/parts from the server\r\n\r\n### Fix 2: Normal SSE flow scenario\r\nAdded `useEffect` in `ToolPart.tsx`:\r\n- Triggers when child session polling stops (child completed + 2.5s settle grace elapsed)\r\n- Fetches only the target message (`part.messageID`) containing the task tool part\r\n- Preserves reference identity to avoid unnecessary re-renders\r\n- Won't overwrite in-flight SSE state for other messages in the parent session\r\n\r\n## Testing\r\n- ✅ Type check: pass\r\n- ✅ Lint: pass  \r\n- ✅ CI: pass",
    "created_at": "2026-04-08T08:09:13Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-09T01:37:47Z",

    "body": "## Summary\n\nProviders and agents now load from global OpenCode config (`~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`) instead of being cached per-project.\n\n**Problem:** Users had to re-configure providers and agents for each project. Different ports also created isolated localStorage caches, making settings appear to change randomly.\n\n**Root causes:**\n1. `directoryScoped` state in `useConfigStore` cached providers/agents per directory, persisted to localStorage\n2. Directory parameter passed to OpenCode SDK caused project-scoped config lookups\n3. Browser localStorage is per-origin (different ports = different storage)\n\n**Changes:**\n- Removed `directoryScoped` caching from `useConfigStore` - providers/agents are now loaded once globally\n- Removed directory parameter from `getProviders()` and `listAgents()` SDK calls\n- Simplified `activateDirectory()` to not reload providers/agents per project\n- Fixed server proxy to strip `content-encoding` header (Bun auto-decompresses responses, causing `ERR_CONTENT_DECODING_FAILED` when header was forwarded)\n\n**Files changed:**\n- `packages/ui/src/stores/useConfigStore.ts` - Removed per-directory caching (~500 lines simplified)\n- `packages/ui/src/stores/useAgentsStore.ts` - Removed directory iteration in config refresh\n- `packages/ui/src/lib/opencode/client.ts` - SDK calls now omit directory parameter\n- `packages/web/server/index.js` - Added `content-encoding` to hop-by-hop headers\n\n## Testing\n\n1. Configure providers/agents in `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`\n2. Open OpenChamber on different ports\n3. Verify providers/agents appear consistently across projects and ports",

    "body": "Close #806 \n\n## User-Facing Impact\n\nFirst-time desktop users now experience a smooth, guided setup with intelligent error recovery:\n\n**What's New:**\n- **Friendly Setup Wizard**: Choose between local install or connect to remote server\n- **Smart Recovery**: Automatically handles connection failures with clear next steps\n- **Seamless Onboarding**: No more CLI polling delays - instant feedback when ready\n\n**Problem Solved:**\nPreviously, users hitting connection issues had no clear path forward. This delivers guided recovery options and prevents getting stuck in broken states.\n\n## What Changed\n\n**Desktop Boot Outcome System:**\n- Desktop-local boot outcome model plus first-launch choice persistence\n- Startup and new-window flows consistently choose between main app, chooser, and recovery states\n- Real desktop first-launch chooser and repair-oriented recovery flows\n- In-session connection settings escape hatches and safer remote connection onboarding\n- Remote host validation and desktop settings persistence improvements\n- Aligned probe retry behavior with blocking invalid navigation targets\n- Redacted sensitive recovery URLs for security\n\n<img width=\"280\" height=\"200\" alt=\"HapiGo_2026-04-05_23 16 31\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0c5d09fc-9415-49b7-8bfb-ab1359871c59\" />\n  \n<img width=\"280\" height=\"200\" alt=\"HapiGo_2026-04-05_23 30 05\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19ec3bd7-0562-4624-9841-4cee2d0b665e\" />\n\n<img width=\"280\" height=\"200\" alt=\"HapiGo_2026-04-05_10 59 16\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4cd0e5eb-4ae2-425a-aea9-1b9ae74eb171\" />\n\n## Test Plan\n- [x] `bun test packages/ui/src/lib/desktopBoot.test.ts`\n- [x] `cargo test --manifest-path packages/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml`\n- [x] `bun run type-check`\n- [x] `bun run lint`\n- [x] `bun run build`\n\n## Manual Follow-up\n\n### Clean First-Launch Test\n```bash\nOPENCHAMBER_DATA_DIR=/tmp/openchamber-test packages/desktop/src-tauri/target/debug/openchamber-desktop\n```\n- [ ] Verify chooser appears with Local Install and Connect Remote tabs\n- [ ] Click \"Connect Remote\" tab → verify NO back button (use tabs to switch)\n- [ ] Test remote connection with valid URL (should restart and connect)\n- [ ] Test remote connection with invalid URL (should show error + action buttons)\n\n### Recovery Mode Test\n- [ ] Verify recovery screen appears when default host is unreachable\n- [ ] Verify \"Connect to a Different Server\" link shows back button (required for navigation)\n- [ ] Verify back to recovery options works correctly\n- [ ] Verify \"Use Local Instead\" switches to local setup\n\n### UI Polish Verification\n- [ ] Verify all first-launch flows complete without CLI auto-polling delays\n- [ ] Verify manual \"Check and Continue\" button detects CLI availability\n- [ ] Verify remote connection probe blocks on wrong-service and unreachable",

    "body": "## Summary\n- add Save actions for files in the main Files view, the floating file viewer toolbar, and the sidebar file picker context menu\n- add `downloadFile` support to the shared files API and wire it up in the web and VS Code runtimes through `/api/fs/raw?download=true`\n- replace macOS-only Finder wording with platform-appropriate labels and fix Windows reveal behavior by launching Explorer through PowerShell so files and folders open reliably\n\n## Testing\n- bun run type-check\n- bun run build\n- manual test of Save and Open in File Explorer from the browser on Windows",

    "body": "## Summary\n\n- In sorted render mode during streaming, text/reasoning/justification parts were hidden until the message completed (`finish === 'stop'`). When the model called the `question` tool, the `QuestionCard` rendered immediately via SSE, but the preceding thinking/justification content was invisible.\n- Adds `hasBlockingToolCall` detection for question tool parts, and bypasses the sorted-mode text/reasoning deferral when the model is paused waiting for user input.\n- Also fixes justification rendering: only skips in flat loop when the activity group will render it, preventing hidden justification when no activity group is active.",

    "body": "Summary\r\nThis PR fixes stale file content in the Files view when a file is changed outside OpenChamber.\r\n\r\nPreviously, if a markdown or text file was already open in the UI and then edited through the filesystem, the open editor could keep showing old content. In practice, users often had to fully reload the page to see the latest file state.\r\n\r\nWhat changed\r\nAdded external-change detection for the currently open file in the Files view.\r\nReload open file content when the file changes on disk and the editor has no unsaved local changes.\r\nUpdated the file-content cache to validate cached entries against file metadata instead of trusting path-only cache hits.\r\nExtended statFile to include mtimeMs so shared UI logic can reliably detect external file updates.\r\nKept web and VS Code file-runtime contracts in sync.\r\nWhy this approach\r\nThe issue had two separate causes:\r\n\r\nThe open file view did not actively detect that the file had changed on disk.\r\nThe runtime file-content cache could return stale content for the same path even after an external edit.\r\nFixing only one of these was not enough. This PR addresses both, while keeping the behavior conservative:\r\n\r\nwe only refresh when the file actually changed\r\nwe do not overwrite unsaved local edits\r\nUser impact\r\nAfter this change, if a file is open in the Files view and is modified externally:\r\n\r\nthe UI picks up the change automatically\r\nclosing and reopening the file shows fresh content\r\na full page reload is no longer required",

    "body": "## Summary\n\nComplete sidebar redesign for cleaner visual hierarchy, better spacing, and improved project management experience.\n\n### Changes\n\n- Added collapsible PROJECTS section header (matches RECENT style)\n- Project labels: uppercase, semibold, letter-spacing for better hierarchy\n- Added space-y-4 between projects for visual clarity\n- Reduced folder name size (12px) and weight\n- Compact header redesign with medium New Chat button\n- Removed diff stats and \"Add Action\" from session rows\n- Limited recent sessions to 48h window\n- Merged upstream: kept multi-run button and project notes panel\n\n### Files Changed (10)\n\n- SidebarProjectsList.tsx - PROJECTS header\n- sortableItems.tsx - Project label styling\n- SessionFolderItem.tsx - Folder name size\n- SidebarHeader.tsx - Header redesign + upstream features\n- SidebarActivitySections.tsx - Section styling\n- SessionSidebar.tsx - Cleanup\n- ConfirmDialogs.tsx - FolderNameDialog\n- SessionNodeItem.tsx - Session cleanup\n- useSessionSidebarSections.ts - Section hooks\n- ProjectActionsButton.tsx - Remove Add Action\n\n### Testing\n- type-check: pass\n- lint: pass\n- Conflicts with #741: resolved",
    "created_at": "2026-03-25T17:46:30Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-02T03:03:30Z",

    "title": "feat(web): add WebSocket transport for message event streaming with SSE fallback",
    "user": {
      "login": "geekifan",
      "id": 74950140,
      "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjc0OTUwMTQw",
      "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/74950140?v=4",
      "gravatar_id": "",
      "url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan",
      "html_url": "https://github.com/geekifan",
      "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan/followers",
      "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan/following{/other_user}",
      "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan/gists{/gist_id}",
      "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan/starred{/owner}{/repo}",
      "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan/subscriptions",
      "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan/orgs",
      "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan/repos",
      "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan/events{/privacy}",
      "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/geekifan/received_events",

    },
    "body": "## Summary\n\nThis PR adds WebSocket support for the UI message event stream while keeping SSE as a supported fallback.\n\nOpenChamber currently relies on SSE for chat and session event streaming. This change adds a WebSocket transport between the browser UI and the OpenChamber web server, while the server still proxies the upstream OpenCode SSE stream. It also adds a user-facing setting to choose `Auto`, `WebSocket`, or `SSE`.\n\n## What changed\n\n- Add message event WebSocket endpoints on the web server:\n  - `/api/global/event/ws`\n  - `/api/event/ws`\n- Keep upstream OpenCode integration on SSE and bridge SSE -> WebSocket on the OpenChamber server\n- Preserve existing OpenChamber synthetic events on the WS path, including:\n  - `openchamber:session-status`\n  - `openchamber:session-activity`\n  - `openchamber:notification`\n  - `openchamber:heartbeat`\n- Add a shared setting: `messageStreamTransport`\n  - `auto`\n  - `ws`\n  - `sse`\n- Add a Chat settings control for choosing the message stream transport\n- Update the UI event client to:\n  - prefer WebSocket in `auto` mode\n  - fall back to SSE when WS fails before becoming ready\n  - keep the existing event handling shape unchanged for consumers\n\n## Notes\n\n- `Auto` mode currently uses an optimistic WS-first strategy and temporarily falls back to SSE if WS fails before the stream becomes ready.",
    "created_at": "2026-03-25T14:52:08Z",

    "title": "feat(i18n): add Vietnamese language support",
    "user": {

      "id": 83950837,
      "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjgzOTUwODM3",
      "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/83950837?v=4",

    "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add complete Vietnamese (`vi`) locale with all translated surfaces\n- Register Vietnamese in i18n config and language selector\n- Extends the i18n infrastructure from #622\n\n> **Note:** This PR is based on #622 (Chinese language support). It should be merged after #622 is merged, or both can be merged together.\n\n## What's translated\n\n- **Settings navigation**: All page titles, group labels, descriptions, home page quick links\n- **Appearance settings**: All section headers, option labels, tooltips, checkboxes\n- **Main navigation tabs**: Chat (Trò chuyện), Plan (Kế hoạch), Diff (So sánh), Files (Tệp), Terminal (Dòng lệnh), Git\n- **Common labels**: Reset (Đặt lại), Back (Quay lại), Close (Đóng)\n\n## Files changed\n\n| File | Change |\n|------|--------|\n| `packages/ui/src/i18n/locales/vi.ts` | New Vietnamese locale (144 translation keys) |\n| `packages/ui/src/i18n/locales/index.ts` | Export vi locale |\n| `packages/ui/src/i18n/index.ts` | Register vi in i18next resources |\n| `packages/ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts` | Add `vi: 'Tiếng Việt'` label |\n| `packages/ui/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts` | Add `vi: 'Tiếng Việt'` label |\n| `OpenChamberVisualSettings.tsx` | Add Vietnamese option to language dropdown |\n\n## Verification\n\n- ✅ `bun run type-check` — passes\n- ✅ `bun run lint` — passes\n- ✅ `bun run build` — passes",
    "created_at": "2026-03-20T06:31:04Z",

    "body": "## Summary\n\nFixes the directory browser being locked to `homeDirectory`, preventing navigation to paths like `/opt`, `/var/www`, or `/srv` on remote servers.\n\n- Set tree `rootDirectory` to `\"/\"` instead of `homeDirectory`\n- Bypass home boundary checks (`isPathWithinHome`, `loadDirectory` guard, entry filtering) when root is `/`\n- Allow `goToParent()` to navigate all the way up to `/`\n- Add 22 unit tests covering all extracted pure logic\n\nCloses btriapitsyn/openchamber#642\n\n## Changes\n\n| File | Change |\n|------|--------|\n| `DirectoryExplorerDialog.tsx` | `rootDirectory=\"/\"`, `isRootReady={true}` |\n| `DirectoryTree.tsx` | `effectiveRoot` accepts `/`; `isPathWithinHome`, `loadDirectory`, entry filters bypass when root=`/` |\n| `useDirectoryStore.ts` | `goToParent()` only stops at `/`, not at home |\n| `__tests__/directory-tree-root.test.ts` | 22 tests for pure logic |\n\n## Testing\n\n```\nbun test packages/ui/src/components/session/__tests__/directory-tree-root.test.ts\n# 22 pass, 0 fail, 42 expect() calls\n```\n\nType-check and lint both pass:\n```\nbun run type-check  # all workspaces pass\nbun run lint        # all workspaces pass\n```",

    "body": "## Summary\n\n- **Keyboard Tools Toggle**: Adds a new setting (Settings → Visual → Mobile Keyboard Tools) to show/hide the mobile keyboard toolbar items (attachments, model selector, agent selector). When hidden, only voice and send/stop buttons remain, providing a cleaner input experience.\n- **iOS PWA Safe Area Optimization**: Fixes inadequate bottom safe area padding on iOS PWA. The `--oc-safe-area-bottom-visual` variable now provides meaningful clearance (~20px on iPhone) for the home indicator instead of the previous negligible ~1.7px. The home indicator blur gradient properly covers the full safe area zone, and keyboard-open padding removes redundant double-padding.\n\n## Changes\n\n| File | Change |\n|------|--------|\n| `useUIStore.ts` | Add `showMobileKeyboardTools` boolean + setter (default: true, persisted) |\n| `OpenChamberVisualSettings.tsx` | Add toggle checkbox in Visual settings (mobile only) |\n| `OpenChamberPage.tsx` | Include `mobileKeyboardTools` in Chat section visible settings |\n| `ChatInput.tsx` | Conditionally render toolbar items based on toggle |\n| `mobile.css` | Optimize safe area visual calc (0.05→0.6, 4px→22px cap), fix blur gradient height, simplify keyboard safe area |\n\n## Verification\n- `bun run type-check` ✅\n- `bun run lint` ✅\n- `bun run build` ✅",

    "body": "## Summary\n\nThis PR adds bundled Chinese (Simplified) UI translations with a persisted language selector, while keeping the implementation scoped to existing UI surfaces.\n\n## User-visible changes\n\n- Add **English / 中文（简体）** language selection in **Appearance** settings\n- Translate core navigation labels:\n  - header tabs: Chat / Plan / Diff / Files / Terminal\n  - right sidebar tabs: Git / Files\n- Translate **Settings** home and navigation labels\n- Translate **Appearance / OpenChamber visual settings** labels, tooltips, and option text\n\n## Implementation approach\n\n- Add `i18next` + `react-i18next` with bundled local resources\n- Initialize i18n once at app startup in `packages/ui/src/main.tsx`\n- Persist selected language in `useUIStore`\n- Keep translations at render boundaries with `t(...)`\n- For module-level settings option arrays, switch from literal `label` / `description` strings to `labelKey` / `descriptionKey` so they can still be declared statically without freezing translated text at module load time\n\n## Files touched\n\n- `packages/ui/src/i18n/index.ts`\n- `packages/ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts`\n- `packages/ui/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts`\n- `packages/ui/package.json`\n- `packages/ui/src/main.tsx`\n- `packages/ui/src/stores/useUIStore.ts`\n- `packages/ui/src/components/layout/Header.tsx`\n- `packages/ui/src/components/layout/RightSidebarTabs.tsx`\n- `packages/ui/src/components/views/SettingsView.tsx`\n- `packages/ui/src/components/sections/openchamber/OpenChamberVisualSettings.tsx`\n- `bun.lock`\n\n## Why `OpenChamberVisualSettings.tsx` is still the largest diff\n\nThat file contains most of the user-facing appearance strings in one place. To make those labels translatable without changing behavior, this PR has to:\n\n- replace many inline literals with `t(...)`\n- convert multiple static option tables to translation keys\n- add the language selector UI\n\nI also aligned the markup more closely with current upstream structure to keep the review focused on i18n instead of unrelated formatting churn.\n\n## Deliberate non-goals\n\nThis PR does **not** attempt to translate everything in the app. It intentionally does **not** touch:\n\n- chat message content\n- notification summarization/content generation\n- server-side strings and API payloads\n- git workflow text beyond the existing translated UI surfaces\n- unrelated UI refactors or style changes\n\n## Verification\n\nValidated on the current branch after rebasing onto latest upstream `main`:\n\n- ✅ `bun run type-check`\n- ✅ `bun run lint`\n- ✅ `bun run build`\n\n## Current diff size\n\nCompared with `upstream/main`:\n\n- 11 files changed\n- ~636 insertions / ~265 deletions\n\nMost of that size is translation data (`en.ts`, `zh-CN.ts`) plus the appearance settings file where the existing visible strings are concentrated.",

    "body": "## Summary\r\n- Add Kanban column automation with runtime state tracking (`running` / `done` / `failed`).\r\n- Trigger automation when a card enters an automated column (on create or move), using the card’s selected worktree.\r\n- Keep board behavior stable while adding automation settings and polling updates.\r\n\r\n## Board features\r\n- Create columns.\r\n- Create cards in any column.\r\n- Configure automation per column (`onEnter` + optional `onFinishMoveTo`).\r\n- Automatically move cards to the next configured column after automation completes.\r\n\r\n## User workflow\r\n1. User creates columns and cards in the board.\r\n2. User configures automation in a column and optionally sets a next column for auto-move.\r\n3. User creates or moves a card into that automated column.\r\n4. A session starts automatically, and the card **title and description are appended to the session message**.\r\n5. Card status updates in the board and, if configured, the card is moved automatically to the next column.\r\n",

    "body": "This pull request introduces major improvements to file handling and media viewing in the UI codebase (fixes #485). The most significant changes are the addition of a flexible `MediaViewer` component, expanded file metadata support (including size and modification time), and a robust, streaming-friendly file upload system with error handling and progress updates. There are also new utility modules for file content invalidation and file downloads, plus enhancements to the API and bridge interfaces to support these features.\r\n\r\n**Media viewing and file metadata:**\r\n\r\n* Added a reusable `MediaViewer` component to display PDF, audio, and video files with appropriate UI and styling options. (`packages/ui/src/components/views/MediaViewer.tsx`)\r\n* Extended file metadata throughout the API and bridge layers to include `size` and `modifiedTime` for files and directories, enabling richer file information in the UI. (`packages/ui/src/lib/api/types.ts`, `packages/ui/src/lib/opencode/client.ts`, `packages/vscode/src/bridge.ts`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-6755a9fdf5205ce63e2378b5934ef0f4dc7380297dc198d31ca5ee333ae8aa67R447-R454) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5be42547721793f51b1b9de790968c67ba76c6ddee76060efb825c421d25f9b2R95-R96) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-6a93313897b2e9b7a23b047fcfec13c89251793d14132cb40fef4b50ad42f1d5R96-R97) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-5be42547721793f51b1b9de790968c67ba76c6ddee76060efb825c421d25f9b2R2017-R2018) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-6a93313897b2e9b7a23b047fcfec13c89251793d14132cb40fef4b50ad42f1d5R838-R899) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-6a93313897b2e9b7a23b047fcfec13c89251793d14132cb40fef4b50ad42f1d5R1590-R1619)\r\n\r\n**File upload and download enhancements:**\r\n\r\n* Implemented a new file upload system supporting both streaming uploads and JSON fallback, with progress reporting, error handling, and size validation. (`packages/ui/src/lib/fileUpload.ts`, `packages/ui/src/lib/fileUploadGuards.ts`, `packages/ui/src/lib/api/types.ts`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-16e750c1cfd618c4d014b14fe6cfe79601c19ecc68ae5809bd81250ca13f98a7R1-R145) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c4661ac228243bcc29035d4ab401f7013356a6df080271aea518ce21c4407b4cR1-R71) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-6755a9fdf5205ce63e2378b5934ef0f4dc7380297dc198d31ca5ee333ae8aa67R482-R517)\r\n* Added a utility for triggering file downloads from the UI by generating a download link for a given file path and name. (`packages/ui/src/lib/fileDownload.ts`)\r\n\r\n**File content invalidation and context guards:**\r\n\r\n* Introduced a module for notifying and subscribing to file content invalidation events, allowing the UI to react to file changes efficiently. (`packages/ui/src/lib/fileContentInvalidation.ts`)\r\n* Improved context file open validation to better handle binary files and media, ensuring that non-text files are routed to appropriate viewers instead of being read as text. (`packages/ui/src/lib/contextFileOpenGuard.ts`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7da7dde713d58fad625f537baac002a62a918a4cde371422f7b9b94a5449a69cR3) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-7da7dde713d58fad625f537baac002a62a918a4cde371422f7b9b94a5449a69cR45-R59)\r\n\r\n**API and bridge interface upgrades:**\r\n\r\n* Expanded the `FilesAPI` and VSCode bridge interfaces to support new operations (`stat`, streaming `uploadFile`, enhanced `writeFile`), and improved directory listing with concurrent metadata fetching for scalability. (`packages/ui/src/lib/api/types.ts`, `packages/vscode/src/bridge.ts`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-6755a9fdf5205ce63e2378b5934ef0f4dc7380297dc198d31ca5ee333ae8aa67R482-R517) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-6a93313897b2e9b7a23b047fcfec13c89251793d14132cb40fef4b50ad42f1d5R838-R899) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-6a93313897b2e9b7a23b047fcfec13c89251793d14132cb40fef4b50ad42f1d5R1590-R1619) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-6a93313897b2e9b7a23b047fcfec13c89251793d14132cb40fef4b50ad42f1d5L1583-R1679)\r\n\r\nThese changes collectively make file management and media viewing more robust, performant, and user-friendly in the UI.",

    "body": "This PR adds agents loops to Openchamber. Agent loops are a coordinated series of sessions automatically running after one another on a shared list of work packages. This is essentially an implementation of the popular Ralph Wiggum pattern, but with much better observability than most scripts can offer.\r\nThese loops can be easily created and controlled via a custom session UI that only applies to loop type sessions.\r\n\r\nThis PR also adds a devcontainer definition file to make working on OpenChamber easier, especially in Github Codespaces.\r\n<img width=\"2191\" height=\"1198\" alt=\"Bildschirmfoto 2026-03-03 um 21 12 20\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fa073a12-f52e-43b6-9eef-13db7d7de1e4\" />\r\n<img width=\"2191\" height=\"1198\" alt=\"Bildschirmfoto 2026-03-03 um 21 12 33\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e7c93650-26c4-45f3-bfbd-017c6f8369e1\" />\r\n<img width=\"2191\" height=\"1198\" alt=\"Bildschirmfoto 2026-03-03 um 21 12 42\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/046f7a80-cc8e-4694-b3f7-6754106bf525\" />\r\n\r\n",

    "title": "Feature: Model-Profiles",
    "user": {
      "login": "vincent-chang",
      "id": 7593578,

      "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7593578?v=4",

      "url": "https://api.github.com/users/vincent-chang",
      "html_url": "https://github.com/vincent-chang",

    "body": "### Summary\r\n---\r\nWhile developing with the oh-my-opencode plugin, I found that different agents often need to be configured with different LLM models. As we all know, models come from different providers, and each provider typically offers different plans, pricing, rate limits, and usage quotas.\r\n\r\nBecause of this, I frequently need to switch not just a single model, but an entire set of agent model configurations (and sometimes even the provider) depending on the current situation—for example: budget constraints, remaining quota, availability, latency, or specific model capabilities.\r\n\r\nThis PR introduces/supports the concept of Model-Profiles: predefined profiles that bundle model (and provider) settings for all agents, allowing users to quickly switch a whole configuration set in one step instead of editing each agent individually.\r\n\r\n### PR Changes\r\n---\r\n1. Added a new configuration page named **“Model Profiles”** to the **Settings** dialog.\r\n\r\n2. In this page, users can **create new profiles** and configure the **model selection per agent** within each profile.\r\n\r\n3. When a profile is saved, its data is persisted to:\r\n   - `~/.config/openchamber/settings.json`\r\n\r\n4. Clicking **“Apply Profile”** applies the selected profile:\r\n   - For **oh-my-opencode**, the configuration is written to:\r\n     - `~/.config/opencode/oh-my-opencode.json`\r\n   - For other agents, the behavior matches the existing agent configuration page (i.e., it updates the agent configuration accordingly).\r\n\r\n### Verification\r\n---\r\n\r\n- bun run type-check\r\n- bun run lint\r\n- bun run build",

    "body": "## Summary\r\n- Add full mobile runtime support across desktop/ui/web with device login flow, mobile-specific UI/layout updates, and native plugin integrations (clipboard/dialog/notifications/haptics/biometric/opener).\r\n- Add mobile branding/icon pipeline for Android and iOS generation and apply deterministic branding patches during init/build.\r\n- Add end-to-end mobile release automation using Fastlane + GitHub Actions (`mobile-release.yml`) with manual dispatch, artifact-only mode, Play internal upload, TestFlight upload, and release workflow integration.\r\n- Add F-Droid style repository generation/publish support plus a complete secrets setup guide for CI (`docs/MOBILE_RELEASE_SECRETS.md`).\r\n\r\n## Validation\r\n- `bun run type-check`\r\n- `bun run lint`\r\n- `bun run build`",
